steps.title: Eliminate the negative-slope choices
steps.content: The scatterplot shows a clear positive trend — as $x$ increases, $y$ increases. Choices C ($y = -0.8x + 3$) and D ($y = -3x + 0.8$) both have negative slopes and predict the opposite trend, so they are eliminated.

steps.title: Compare the two positive-slope choices at a data point
steps.content:
The rightmost data point is near $(8, 26)$.
Choice B predicts $y = 0.8(8) + 3 = 9.4$ — far below the actual value.
Choice A predicts $y = 3(8) + 0.8 = 24.8$ — very close to 26.
Choice A also matches the leftmost point near $(1, 4)$: $y = 3(1) + 0.8 = 3.8$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
